title: Access Teknic SD motor software and prepare to save custom motor parameters
summary: Obtain the Teknic manufacturer application and connect to a Teknic SD motor so the motor's custom parameters can be accessed and saved before motor or gearbox replacement work. The source states this applies to Z-axis and A-axis motors mounted into gearboxes and identifies the required software selections as ClearPath, SD motor type, and NEMA 23/34 IP53 Motor Body.
when_to_use: Use this procedure before Teknic SD motor or related gearbox replacement work when custom motor parameters must be accessed and saved from a Z-axis or A-axis motor.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a complete motor removal or replacement procedure.
Do not use this runbook when detailed connection sequence, save dialog workflow, or recovery steps are required; the source does not provide those details.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Gather the listed tools and connection hardware, identify the Teknic SD motor, obtain the manufacturer software, connect to the motor, and access and save the custom parameters.

safety_notes: Use safety glasses and gloves as listed in the broader motor replacement context referenced by the candidate.
This task is embedded in motor replacement work and involves powered connections and replacement-related work; use a higher safe role.
The source packet does not provide a detailed electrical safety, power-up, or lockout sequence for this software access task.

system_preconditions: A Teknic SD motor to be worked on has been identified.
The task is being performed in preparation for motor or gearbox replacement work.
Required connection hardware and power supplies are available.

visual_references.description: Figure 7-5 identifying the Teknic SD motor referenced by the software download and parameter access steps.
Page 122 image likely showing the motor USB access point or USB port cover used for configuration transfer.
Gearbox figure that may help distinguish the motor from the gearbox assembly in related service context.
Additional gearbox figure that may help distinguish gearbox-related hardware from the Teknic SD motor.

steps.title: Gather required tools and connection hardware
steps.purpose: Ensure all listed equipment needed to connect to the motor and run the manufacturer software is available before beginning.
steps.instruction: Gather the listed items needed for this task: laptop, USB-A to micro-USB cable, 25-V power supply, 75-V power supply, 4-pin Molex power supply connector, and 8-pin Molex communication cable.
steps.expected_result: All listed tools and connection hardware are available for the software access task.
steps.healthy_condition: The laptop, cables, power supplies, and Molex connectors listed by the source are present and ready for use.
steps.failure_condition: One or more required items are missing or unavailable.

actions.label: Verify required items
actions.exact_action: Gather the listed items needed for this task: laptop, USB-A to micro-USB cable, 25-V power supply, 75-V power supply, 4-pin Molex power supply connector, and 8-pin Molex communication cable.

stop_or_escalate_if: Required connection hardware or power supplies are not available.
The technician cannot confirm the correct equipment for connecting to the motor.

source_evidence.text: Gather the listed items needed for this task: laptop, USB-A to micro-USB cable, 25-V power supply, 75-V power supply, 4-pin Molex power supply connector, and 8-pin Molex communication cable.

Nearby text states that each motor has custom parameters that should be saved by connecting to the motor and running the manufacturer application. Related terms include ... laptop, USB-A to micro-USB cable, 25-V power supply, 75-V power supply, 4-pin Molex power supply connector, and 8-pin Molex communication cable.

steps.title: Identify the Teknic SD motor to be serviced
steps.purpose: Confirm the correct motor is being prepared for parameter access and distinguish it from the gearbox assembly.
steps.instruction: Identify the Teknic SD motor to be worked on. This section applies to Z-axis and A-axis motors and notes that these motors are mounted into gearboxes which may also be replaced.
steps.expected_result: The correct Teknic SD motor has been identified for the parameter backup task.
steps.healthy_condition: The technician can visually identify the Teknic SD motor and distinguish it from the associated gearbox assembly.
steps.failure_condition: The motor cannot be confidently identified or distinguished from the gearbox assembly.

actions.label: Identify motor
actions.exact_action: Identify the Teknic SD motor to be worked on; the section notes this applies to Z-axis and A-axis motors and that these motors are mounted into gearboxes which may also be replaced.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use Figure 7-5 to identify the Teknic SD motor.
Use the gearbox figure as optional context to distinguish the motor from gearbox-related hardware.
Use the gearbox figure as optional context to distinguish the motor from gearbox-related hardware.

stop_or_escalate_if: The correct Z-axis or A-axis Teknic SD motor cannot be identified.
The technician cannot distinguish the motor from the gearbox assembly.

steps.title: Connect to the motor
steps.purpose: Establish the physical connection required so the motor's custom parameters can be accessed by the manufacturer application.
steps.instruction: Connect a cable to the motor so the motor's custom parameters can be accessed.
steps.expected_result: A cable connection to the motor is established for software access.
steps.healthy_condition: The motor is connected in a way that allows the manufacturer application to access its custom parameters.
steps.failure_condition: The motor cannot be connected or the connection does not allow parameter access.

actions.label: Connect cable to motor
actions.exact_action: Connect a cable to the motor so the motor's custom parameters can be accessed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the page 122 image as recommended visual support for locating the motor USB access point or USB port cover.

stop_or_escalate_if: The connection point cannot be located.
The custom parameters cannot be accessed after connection.

source_evidence.text: Connect a cable to the motor so the motor's custom parameters can be accessed.

The source explicitly states that custom parameters can be accessed by connecting a cable to the motor and running the manufacturer application, but it does not provide detailed connection sequence or save dialog steps.

Nearby text describes motor replacement workflow, including ... connecting a USB cable to the motor and laptop, running the Teknic ClearPath application, and using File > Save Configuration to create an .mtr file. The image is likely related to the labeled USB PORT COVER and may help technicians locate the motor USB access point needed to transfer configuration parameters.

steps.title: Open the Teknic download site
steps.purpose: Reach the manufacturer download page needed to obtain the application used for motor parameter access.
steps.instruction: Open the manufacturer download site at https://teknic.com/downloads/ on the laptop.
steps.expected_result: The Teknic downloads page is open on the laptop.
steps.healthy_condition: The technician can access the Teknic downloads page.
steps.failure_condition: The site cannot be reached or opened.

actions.label: Open download site
actions.exact_action: Open the manufacturer download site at https://teknic.com/downloads/ on the laptop.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Teknic download site cannot be reached.
The technician does not have access to the download site.

source_evidence.text: Open the manufacturer download site at https://teknic.com/downloads/ on the laptop.
It provides the download site and the required software selections: ClearPath, SD motor type, and NEMA 23/34 IP53 Motor Body.

steps.title: Select the required software options
steps.purpose: Choose the manufacturer software options specified by the source for the Teknic SD motor.
steps.instruction: In the download selections, choose "ClearPath," then "SD" for the motor type, and then "NEMA 23/34 IP53 Motor Body."
steps.expected_result: The correct software selection path has been chosen on the manufacturer site.
steps.healthy_condition: The selections match ClearPath > SD > NEMA 23/34 IP53 Motor Body.
steps.failure_condition: The required selections are unavailable, unclear, or do not match the source.

actions.label: Choose software options
actions.exact_action: In the download selections, choose "ClearPath," then "SD" for the motor type, and then "NEMA 23/34 IP53 Motor Body."

stop_or_escalate_if: The required software options cannot be found on the manufacturer site.
The available selections do not match ClearPath, SD, and NEMA 23/34 IP53 Motor Body.

source_evidence.text: In the download selections, choose "ClearPath," then "SD" for the motor type, and then "NEMA 23/34 IP53 Motor Body."
It provides the download site and the required software selections: ClearPath, SD motor type, and NEMA 23/34 IP53 Motor Body.

Nearby text states that each motor has custom parameters that should be saved by connecting to the motor and running the manufacturer application. The manual references the Teknic download site and instructs users to select ClearPath, SD motor type, and NEMA 23/34 IP53 Motor Body.

steps.title: Run the manufacturer application and save parameters
steps.purpose: Use the manufacturer application to access the motor's custom parameters and save them before replacement work.
steps.instruction: Run the manufacturer application to access and save the motor's custom parameters.
steps.expected_result: The manufacturer application is running and the motor's custom parameters are accessible for saving.
steps.healthy_condition: The application can access the motor and the custom parameters can be saved.
steps.failure_condition: The application cannot access the motor or the custom parameters cannot be saved.

actions.label: Run application and save parameters
actions.exact_action: Run the manufacturer application to access and save the motor's custom parameters.

stop_or_escalate_if: The manufacturer application cannot be run.
The motor's custom parameters cannot be accessed.
The motor's custom parameters cannot be saved.

success_criteria: The Teknic manufacturer application is obtained from the specified download site.
The correct software selections are used: ClearPath, SD motor type, and NEMA 23/34 IP53 Motor Body.
The Teknic SD motor is connected so its custom parameters are accessible.
The motor's custom parameters are saved before replacement work.
healthy_conditions: The correct Teknic SD motor is identified.
Required tools, cables, and power supplies are available.
The Teknic download site is reachable.
The manufacturer application runs and can access the motor parameters.
failure_conditions: Required tools or connection hardware are missing.
The correct motor cannot be identified.
The manufacturer site or application cannot be accessed.
The custom parameters cannot be accessed or saved.
escalation_guidance: If the motor cannot be connected or the custom parameters cannot be accessed or saved, escalate for higher-level support.
Escalate when the source's missing details prevent safe or successful completion, including missing connection sequence or save workflow details.
